steps.title: Recognize the pattern that shows f(1) directly
steps.content: To display $f(1) = k$ directly as the coefficient without any calculation, we need the exponent to equal 0 when $x = 1$. This is because any nonzero number raised to the power 0 equals 1, so: $$f(1) = \text{coefficient} \times (1.4)^0 = \text{coefficient} \times 1 = \text{coefficient}$$

steps.title: Find the exponent form (x - 1)
steps.content: We need an exponent that equals 0 at $x = 1$. If the exponent is $(x - 1)$, then at $x = 1$ we get $1 - 1 = 0$. Scanning the choices, choice C is the only one with this form: $f(x) = 98(1.4)^{x-1}$.

steps.title: Verify the answer
steps.content: Substituting $x = 1$ into choice C: $$f(1) = 98(1.4)^{1-1} = 98(1.4)^0 = 98 \times 1 = 98$$ The coefficient 98 directly gives the value of $f(1) = k$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
